entry.title: Production Group Planning in IBP
entry.content:
Hi,

I am currently testing the Production Group Planning in SAP IBP but I have a query how will I link the Production Group Attribute with the Product ID? I cannot find a document on how to set it up.

I am trying to test if the Production Group Planning will be the solution to our concern which is we have multiple products that is running in the same production resource but IBP should be able to group the products based on can sizes. Production receipt of products with different can sizes in the same production resource should not be allowed.

Have anyone already tried this new functionality? Hoping for your inputs.

Regards,

J

entry.title: Summary: Kaffee-Ecke zu Change ManagerInnen im SAP-Umfeld: Anforderungen, Skills und Weiterbildung
entry.content:
In der jüngsten Kaffee-Ecke am 20.03.2025 stand das Thema Change ManagerInnen im SAP-Umfeld: Anforderungen, Skills und Weiterbildung im Fokus. Dr. Torben Mauch teilte spannende Einblicke zu Anforderungen, Fähigkeiten und Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten für Change Manager:Innen aus seiner -Erfahrung und diskutierte mit den Teilnehmenden.

Torben erläuterte, dass Change Management darauf abzielt, Organisationen strukturiert von einem Ist- zu einem Soll-Zustand zu führen. Dabei integriert es verschiedene Disziplinen wie strategische Planung, Projektmanagement und Organisationsentwicklung (Definiton nach ACMP – Association of Change Management Professionals).

Das Hauptziel besteht darin, die Akzeptanz von Veränderungen zu fördern und Mitarbeitende beim Übergang zu unterstützen, was langfristig zu einer Steigerung von Effizienz und Effektivität führt.

Das Change Management bietet Ansätze, Tools und Techniken, um den gewünschten zukünftigen Zustand der Organisation erfolgreich zu erreichen. Dabei werden verschiedene Disziplinen integriert wie strategische Planung, Projektmanagement, Organisationsentwicklung, Personalentwicklung und Prozessverbesserung. Hauptziel der Maßnahmen ist die Förderung der Akzeptanz der Veränderung, wobei der Erfolgsfaktor ein reibungsloser Übergang zum Sollzustand ist.

Das Profil eines erfolgreichen Change Management Professionals zeichnet sich durch ein breites Ausbildungsspektrum aus. Um das zu veranschaulichen, zeigte Torben typische Stellenanzeigen für Change ManagerInnen: Neben einem Studium in Wirtschaft, Psychologie oder verwandten Disziplinen sind relevante Zertifizierungen (Prosci, ACMP, etc.) und Berufserfahrung in Veränderungsprozessen von großer Bedeutung. Zudem sind ausgeprägte Kommunikations- und Moderationsfähigkeiten sowie Erfahrungen mit agilen Methoden gefordert, sowie eine schnelle Auffassungsgabe und pragmatisches Vorgehen. Letzteres steht in wahrscheinlich allen Stellenbeschreibungen.

Die größten Überschneidungspunkte im Change Management bei SAP Projekten sieht Torben in den Bereichen Prozess Management, Trainings Management, Projekt Management, Unternehmensstrategie und -entwicklung sowie Personalentwicklung.

Da in der Community viele Change MangerInnen vertreten sind, fragte Torben nach deren Grundausbildung. Die Antworte fielen sehr vielfältig aus (siehe Bild). Torbens Fazit dazu war, dass eine breite Ausbildungspalette als Basis für spätere Change Manger:Innen dienen kann. Da wir uns im Kontext von SAP IT Projekten bewegen, sehen wir auch einige technische Ausbildungen.

Im Anschluss daran befragte Torben die Anwesenden nach Zusatzqualifikationen im Bereich Change Management und auch hier waren die Antworten sehr vielfältig:

  • Projektmanagement IPMA, PMP, GPM
  • Change Manager TÜV
  • (Systemisches) Coaching / Therapieausbildung
  • ITIL / SAFE / CBPP / SCRUM / Prozessmanagement Zertifizierung
  • Change Kurse / Fernstudium
  • PROSCI
  • Erfahrung, Bücher, Podcast, Keynotes
  • Design Thinking
  • SAP Activate
  • SAP Change Basecamp
  • uvm

Tobens Fazit dazu war, dass es analog zum SAP Berater, der in MM oder PP spezialisiert ist, es auch im Change Management unterschiedliche Schwerpunkte und Blickwinkel gibt.

Im Anschluss diskutierten wir die notwendigen Hard Skills für Change ManagerInnen. Torben betonte die Wichtigkeit von Projektmanagement-Kenntnissen. IT-Kenntnisse, SAP-Erfahrung und Change-Management-Zertifikate standen zur Diskussion. Er ließ uns per Mentimeter einschätzen, welche Wichtigkeit wir den einzelnen Skills beimessen.

 

Interessant war, dass SAP Erfahrung und IT Kenntnisse in der Umfrage einen ähnlichen Stellenwert bekamen wie z.B. Change Management Zertifikate. Wir diskutierten kontrovers zum obigen Ergebnis die Relevanz der SAP Erfahrung in SAP Projekten. So betonte Thomas, dass man zwar nicht Abap programmieren können müsse, aber das Verständnis von SAP Technologie und SAP Projekten als Hintergrundwissen sehr wichtig sei.

Im Anschluss machten wir eine weitere Umfrage zu den Soft Skills:

 

 

Dabei zeigte sich, dass alle Soft Skills hoch gerankt wurden: kommunikative und methodische Kompetenzen an erster Stelle. Belastbarkeit, Teamfähigkeit und Selbständigkeit folgen aber direkt danach.

Torben teilte abschließend noch eine Menge an Links zur Weiterbildung im Change Management, die am Ende des Blogposts  zu finden sind.

Diskussion zu Change Management Skills und Weiterbildungen

In der anschließenden Diskussion setzen wir direkt bei den Soft Skills an: Dabei wurde die Wichtigkeit von Empathie und emotionale Intelligenz betont, um die Herausforderungen des Change Managements zu meistern.

Die Fähigkeit, komplexe Situationen schnell zu erfassen und im Team zu arbeiten, wurde ebenfalls als kritisch eingestuft.

Ein interessanter Aspekt, der in der Diskussion aufkam, war das Konzept des T-Shape Profils. Hierbei verfügen die Change ManagerInnen über ein breites Grundwissen in vielen Bereichen, spezialisieren sich aber in einem bestimmten Gebiet. Dies ermöglicht es, flexibel auf verschiedene Situationen zu reagieren und gleichzeitig tiefgreifende Expertise einzubringen.

Torben betonte die Wichtigkeit des Verständnisses für Organisationsstrukturen und ‑prozesse, um erfolgreiches Change Management zu machen.

Dabei wurde darauf hingewiesen, dass Kommunikation oft mit Information verwechselt wird. Effektives Stakeholder Management und Management Support („Lametta von oben“) wurden auch hervorgehoben. Die Bereitschaft, zu reisen wurde kontrovers diskutiert. Gerade vor Ort sein und ein Gefühl für die Situation und die Menschen zu entwickeln ist wichtig. In Bezug auf hybride Arbeit müssen sich Change ManagerInnen der Organisation anpassen – dennoch bleibt es in Präsenz deutlich einfacher, Emotionen und Stimmungen wahrzunehmen.  

Die Diskussion zeigte auch, dass sich das Change Management durch KI und Digitalisierung in einem starken Wandel befindet. Die zunehmende globale Delivery von Change-Projekten und das Potenzial für KI-unterstützte Tools werden die Arbeit von Change ManagerInnen in den kommenden Jahren stark beeinflussen.

Gleichzeitig entsteht ein wachsender Bedarf an Change Management für KI-Transformationen in Unternehmen.

Besonders betont wurde der Wert praktischer Erfahrungen in Veränderungsprojekten. Die lebhafte Diskussion verdeutlichte, dass Change Management eine vielseitige Disziplin ist, die sich ständig weiterentwickelt. Trotz zunehmender Technologisierung bleibt der menschliche Faktor zentral.

Erfolgreiche Change ManagerInnen zeichnen sich dadurch aus, dass sie fachliches Know-how mit ausgeprägten Soft Skills kombinieren und sich flexibel an neue Herausforderungen anpassen.

Vielen Dank an Torben für seine wertvollen Einblicke und an alle Teilnehmenden für ihre engagierten Beiträge zur Diskussion.

Die nächste Kaffee-Ecke wird sich dem Thema SAP PoC - Der Einsatz von KI bei der Erstellung von Trainingsbedarfsanalysen und Lerninhalten widmen. Interessierte können sich zur Kaffee Ecke hier anmelden: https://go4.events.sap.com/eur-kaffee-ecke-april/de_de/home.html

Das SAP Learning and Adoption Forum, unser jährliches Community Event, steht auch wieder in den Startblöcken – ihr könnt euch hier anmelden: https://go4.events.sap.com/eur-learning-adoption-forum/de_de/home.html

Hier die weiterführenden inhaltliche LINKS

Organisationales Change Management Kurse - SAP

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discover-organizational-change-management-for-sap-cloud-projects

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/acting-as-professional-change-manager-in-sap-cloud-projects

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/managing-changes-with-organizational-flexibility-in-sap-s-4hana

https://training.sap.com/course/s4cm01-vernderungsmanagement-erlernen-und-anwenden-am-praxisnahen-beispiel-classroom-001-de-en/?

Organisationales Change Management – Allgemein

https://www.linkedin.com/learning/search?entityType=COURSE&keywords=change%20management&language=de_DE&promptType=LEARNER_TYPED&u=2037868 (LinkedIn Learning)

https://www.blinkist.com/de (Blinkist)

https://www.acmpglobal.org/ (unterschiedliche Basisausbildungen)

https://podcast.opensap.info/education-newscast/ (Podcast zu Change, Training und AI)

SAP Projektmanagement:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-activate

Projektmanagement allgemein

https://www.pmi.org/certifications/certified-associate-capm

https://www.gpm-ipma.de/weiterbildung-mit-zertifikat/projektmanagement/gpm-basislevel

entry.title: CDC - Other consent statement (not mandatory) is becoming inactive.
entry.content:
We have created a Other consent statement (which is not mandatory) and its active. We have added a check box in registration completion screen-set and we have made isConsentGranted as not required for the schema.

 

When we save screen-set with isConsentGranted as not required then Other consent statement is becoming inactive.

Note:  Other consent statement is not mandatory, we have present to customer, he can chose not to accept.

entry.title: Re: How to configure unauthenticated access to SAP CAP Se...
entry.content: Thank you for your help, I tried it that way, but unfortunately the result did not change. Do you have any other ideas or a running sample on Git etc.?

entry.title: Inheritance of responsible cost center in project builder
entry.content:
Hi All, 

We need to inherit reponsible cost center automatically from top WBS element to sub-wbs element . Currently this field is made mandatory for all level of WBS element . Once we enter cost center at top level wbs , it should automatically inherit to sub wbs element . Can any one please suggest any BAPI / API for this requirement or can it be addressed through configuration ?

Thanking you .

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: SAP info structure change synchronous updating
entry.content: Hi Dominik, there is nothing. I looked in every activity and there are many other info structures but not S508. Do you have any other idea how I can change it?

entry.title: Re: Unable to create delivery in public cloud
entry.content:
Error solved, as we have created some custom field in sales order it was having material conversion formula after removing that formula there was no issue in creating the delivery.

Thank you, team,

entry.title: Re: Inheritance of responsible cost center in project bui...
entry.content:
Hello,

Absolutely, Mohammed. You're looking to automate the inheritance of a responsible cost center from a top-level WBS element to its sub-WBS elements in SAP Project System (PS). Here's a breakdown of how to achieve this, focusing on BAPIs/APIs and configuration:

1. Configuration Approach (Recommended for Simplicity):

User Exit or Business Transaction Event (BTE):
This is generally the most straightforward and maintainable approach.
User Exit: Look for user exits within the WBS element creation or change transactions (e.g., CJ20N). You can write ABAP code within the user exit to:
Check if the current WBS element is a sub-WBS element.
Retrieve the responsible cost center from the parent (top-level) WBS element.
Populate the responsible cost center field in the sub-WBS element.
BTE: Business Transaction Events are similar to user exits, and offer a better, standardized way to hook into standard SAP process.
Derivation Rules (If Applicable):
In some cases, depending on your SAP version and configuration, you might be able to use derivation rules. However, this is less likely to be applicable for cost center inheritance within WBS elements directly without custom ABAP development.
Project Profile Configuration:
While you cannot directly configure inheritance, you can use the project profile to define default values. But this would be a static default value, and not a dynamic inheritance from parent.
2. BAPI/API Approach (More Complex, but Flexible):

BAPI_BUS2001_SAVE_MULTIPLE:
This BAPI is used to create or change WBS elements.
You can use it in conjunction with custom ABAP code to:
Read the responsible cost center from the top-level WBS element.
Construct the necessary data structures to update the sub-WBS elements with the inherited cost center.
Call BAPI_BUS2001_SAVE_MULTIPLE to apply the changes.
Function Modules:
There are function modules for reading and changing WBS element data. You could use these to read the top level cost center, and then update the child WBS elements.
Examples of function modules that could be used are those within the function group CNCI.
Enhancement Framework:
Use the enhancement framework to add your own logic to the WBS element creation or change process.
Implementation Steps (General Outline):

Identify the Trigger Point:
Determine when the inheritance should occur (e.g., when a sub-WBS element is created, when the top-level WBS element's cost center is changed).
Retrieve the Parent Cost Center:
Write ABAP code to navigate the WBS hierarchy and retrieve the responsible cost center from the parent (top-level) WBS element.
Update the Sub-WBS Elements:
Use either a BAPI or function module to update the responsible cost center field in the sub-WBS elements.
Error Handling:
Implement robust error handling to manage situations where the parent WBS element doesn't exist, the cost center is invalid, or the update fails.
Testing:
Thoroughly test the implementation to ensure that the inheritance works as expected in all scenarios.
Recommendation:   e-zpassnj com

Configuration (User Exit/BTE) is generally preferred due to its simplicity and maintainability. It avoids the complexities of direct BAPI calls and provides a more integrated approach.
The BAPI method is more complex to implement, and requires a lot of testing, but it can be more flexible.

 

Best Regards

entry.title: Minimum bandwidth requirement for conducting a full-fledged exam - SuccessFactors LMS
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

As per SAP’s initial estimate, the bandwidth requirement for conducting exams in SuccessFactors LMS was stated as 300 kbps per user. Based on this, our client’s IT team arranged the necessary bandwidth. However, users encountered disruptions during a recent pilot exam.

Further investigation revealed that a minimum bandwidth of 5 to 10 Mbps per user is necessary for a seamless exam experience. Additionally, after logging in, the content on the first two pages consumed 17 Mbps and 16 Mbps, respectively.

The client is now requesting the actual minimum bandwidth requirement for a full-scale exam. Based on past experience, what bandwidth should we propose to ensure an optimal exam experience for users?

Appreciate your insights.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: PO Status in Manage Purchase Order
entry.content:
Hi,

correct this is not fully automated. Another solution would be to read the supplier invoice with reference to the PO: https://api.sap.com/api/API_SUPPLIERINVOICE_PROCESS_SRV/resource/Item_with_Purchase_Order_Reference

Regards
Johannes

entry.title: Re: Attachments service on S4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
From your Query it is very evident that you are more concerned about the actual content of the attachment . 
For the same HDM provides an attachment download option via the get API , the URL for which has been posted here. 
https://api.sap.com/api/API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV/resource/get_AttachmentHarmonizedOperationSet_LogicalDocument___LogicalDocument___ArchiveDocumentID___ArchiveDocumentID___LinkedSAPObjectKey___LinkedSAPObjectKey___SAPObjectType___SAPObjectType___HarmonizedDocumentType___HarmonizedDocumentType___

You would be getting a URL and  a valid token for the download of files.  
Also Please note it is highly insecure to provide a content download option directly in API parameters from any kind of document. the above approach provides the flexibility to even handle larger files as well.

entry.title: Join the Eurelectric Digitopia Hub hosted by SAP in Rotterdam
entry.content:
The power industry is at a turning point in its journey towards decarbonisation and greater electrification. Managing the complexities of more renewable energy resources (RES) and distributed energy resources (DERs) connecting to existing medium- and low-voltage electricity grids necessitates enabling technologies and digitalisation that ensure supply continues to meet demand – and vice-versa – effectively and flexibly. 

Digital technologies are game-changing for the power industry, presenting immense opportunities of increased efficiencies, improved maintenance, and better use of electricity. On the flip side, there are risks and challenges facing this digital transformation such as high costs, cyber threats, and market uncertainties. Yet resilient and modern grid networks are central to achieving Europe’s net-zero emission objectives.   

Let’s take the opportunity to come together and discuss in a peer-to-peer format how the industry can leverage digital tools and flexibilities to secure stable flows of electricity from clean sources of energy in Europe.

We are delighted to announce that SAP is the host of the next Eurelectric Digitopia Hub meeting: 

Thursday | 10 April 2025 | Day 3 of SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 
Rotterdam Ahoy | Ahoyweg 10, 3084 BD Rotterdam, Netherlands

Join us at the Digitopia Hub, where Eurelectric is thrilled to present its latest study “Redefining Energy Security in the Age of Electricity” (you can access here) with two practical case studies on grid resilience and cyber resilient virtual power plants (VPPs). The current president of Eurelectric – E.ON – will also share its insights from its recent Energy Playbook on the role of digitalisation and flexibility in optimising grid investments. 

SAP will also invite attendees to immerse themselves at the SAP Pop-Up Campus part of the Conference, where participants can experience an environment featuring a massive 180° LED screen that brings complex concepts and solutions to life with stunning clarity and impact. We will deep dive into “The Future of Distributed Energy Generation: Take a look into S.ENERGY in 2028 and discover how they will serve their customers after a successful SAP S/4HANA Utilities and DER platform implementation”

The Digitopia Hub meeting is open to all interested attendees of the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference as well as members of Eurelectric. If you and/or a representative of your company would like to participate, please send an email to Augustin Chabrol at by 4 April.

We look forward to welcoming you in Rotterdam.
Let’s shape a resilient, affordable, and decarbonised future together!

entry.title: Re: how to create in SAP C4C a report that measures the d...
entry.content: First of all thank you very much. But I am not clear on points 3 and 4. What are the formulas to create them? Thank you very much!

entry.title: Re: How to change the relationship fields between table in query designer
entry.content: Hi, You've recently posted a few questions on a very simple dashboard (# of goals and weight). And you might have a tendency to overcomplicate this task due to lack of Story reporting knowledge. If you continue working with the Story reports it's better to invest more time in studies and either read the Guidebook or pass a certification/reporting learning path. In this case, start with Basic user information, link Goals (Left outer join if you want to see in the outcome aslo employees without goals, Inner join, if you need only empoloyees with goals). Use Objective ID to create a measure on Number of goals in the Designer and use Weight as a measure to add it to you table so it could be summarized.

entry.title: Re: Story Report in SuccessFactors- I am trying to get co...
entry.content: Only Employee name and Job info should be as a Dimension added to the rows. Into columns you must add 2 measures: Weight (can come as a measure from the Query) and Number of goals (it's a custom measure that you need to create: Use type of calculation "Aggregation" -> Count dimension by Objective ID.

entry.title: Re: VF04 - billing due list - What is the difference between billing date FKDAT and V_FKDAT in the A
entry.content: I have a requirement to use FKDAT instead of V_FKDAT if filled in the selection screen

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Creating a custom AI agent on the BTP
entry.content:
@Vitaliy-R Most of blogs and tutorials related to Generative AI are Python based. I am personally also more comfortable with the Python language than with Node.js, which I find more complex.
In other side, I would like to use SAPUI5 for the frontend and CDS to expose data from SAP Backends. So, the way to go would be CAP Node.js app.
So, if I would found a clear and documented Agentic AI scenario template fully based on CAP Node.js using JS GenAI SDK and LangGraph.js , I would certainly go for it. But I never saw such example until now. That's why, I was asking on the possibility to mix python and node.js .

entry.title: Re: Auto generate text for a field
entry.content:
Hi @RishabhGupta198 

I think you shall able to achieve this requirement by using concatenate function, 

a. Add If condition for relationship type "Child" is equal to null then concatenate name string + "A"

b. Add Else If condition for relationship type "Child" is not equal to null then concatenate name string + "B"

Above condition may work until for 2 children. From third child onwards, need to explore logic.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Joule - Joule and EC Quick Actions
entry.content:
Thankyou for your blog @jenny_geipel 

I have a question. Will there be use cases for mass updates in future? (example: extend contract end date of several employees) Or is this something that needs to be handed via BTP?

Experience the empowerment through an in-person event. Network with experts to accelerate your implementations and efficiently operate your SAP landscape using SAP’s ALM portfolio. A great opportunity to engage with SAP's ALM experts and product management team.

Please click here for registration!

NOTE: This is an in-person event and is free to attend.

entry.title: Performance based equipment task list with multiple intervals
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to create a performance-based maintenance plan (running hours) for an equipment task list.

Is it possible to create one maintenance strategy that can be used on multiple equipment’s all with different hourly intervals? So basically a maintenance strategy with all the possible hourly intervals in it.

Furthermore, if I create an equipment task list (IA01) and open the maintenance package overview. There are only 2 digits to display the interval of the maintenance plan.

Is this even possible? Or is there a better way to implement performance-based maintenance plans? I use SAP version 770

entry.title: Re: HTTP Status 500-Internal Server Error in SAP BusinessObjects Intelligence Platform and Data Serv
entry.content:
Hi 

Could you please confirm the following:

1. SAP product version

2. What steps you carried out?

3. Share the error description and screenshots if possible.

Thanks

Abhishek Sinha

Jerome

entry.title: Re: Massive add of language key in gl account SAP PUBLIC...
entry.content:
Hello again Youssef,

And thank you for providing more details about the topic.

So if I understand you well, you have added the "FR" key language into the G/L account master data and even when you have used the Renumber G/L accounts app to change the general description, it hasn't worked isn't it ?

I'm asking you the question because I don't get the same system behaviour than you  :

Manage G/L Account Master Data :

Renumber G/L accounts :

Thank you in advance for your answer.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Massive add of language key in gl account SAP PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content: Hello aoubiyoussef. About ur Pb. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is no direct way to perform mass modification of G/L account translations to add language keys in French. However, there are a few potential approaches you could consider: 01-Chart of Accounts Renumbering process: This process has the capability to change descriptions in multiple languages in one go. You could use the "Renumber G/L Accounts" app and the "Convert Renumbered G/L Accounts" app to achieve this. 02-Migration Cockpit: While primarily used for data migration, this tool might be able to handle mass updates of G/L account translations. 03-Business Process Automation (BPA): This could potentially be used to automate the process of adding French translations to G/L accounts. It's important to note that the options available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition are more limited compared to on-premise versions. The public cloud edition prioritizes standardization and automation, with SAP managing technical operations, upgrades, and system maintenance. If these options don't meet your needs, you may need to consider creating accounts individually using the "Manage Operating Chart of Accounts" app. Kind regards.

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content: @Deepshika18 The registration is closed and seats are full. I will reach out in case we have some withdrawals in registration.

entry.title: Re: SAP info structure change synchronous updating
entry.content: You can change it directly in the TMC4 table with the SE16 transaction.

entry.title: Re: HU height calculation
entry.content:
Hi prakashreddy_r,

your question is not clear for me.

As far as I know in S/4HANA at ERP side the handling unit (LO-HU) does not offer such a functionality.

The packing instruction contains a numeric field "Stacking Factor":

  • 0: HU is not stackable
  • ≥ 1: Number of HUs that can be stacked on to

But this is only for information purpose and not used in logistic processes at ERP side.

Maybe this feature is EWM related (Extended Warehouse Management).

Best regards,

Barış

entry.title: Re: How to concatenate value in Global Variable
entry.content:
Hi @robertot4s.

I have tried by Creating the property. But it is the same and the variable is reset after one splitter is finished working. For the another option, join+gather, do I have to add after the groovy script? Could you pls give advice to me?

Thanks in advance.

Pyae

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Job Scheduler for specific tenant
entry.content: Hello @CarlosRoggan ,

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Job Scheduler for specific tenant
entry.content: Hello @CarlosRoggan,

entry.title: Alternative Movement Types for Asset Scrapping in SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Question: We are currently scrapping assets in SAP by transferring them to a designated scrap location using Movement Type 551 (Scrap Issue) via MB1A / MIGO.

Are there other movement types that can be used for asset scrapping in Inventory Management?

Additionally, should we explicitly list all possible movement types in our documentation, or would it be best to leave it open-ended for flexibility?

Would appreciate insights on best practices and any alternative movement types that might be relevant in specific scenarios.

thanks in advance!!

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Job Scheduler for specific tenant
entry.content: Hello @CarlosRoggan, we have the same problem. As you wrote the blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/using-job-scheduler-in-sap-btp-7-multitenancy-0-intro/ba-p/13511866, maybe you could help us? Thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow
entry.content:
Join the SAP ALM Roadshow and learn to shape your ALM Strategy, manage your on-premise and cloud, hybrid landscapes and get the most value from your SAP solutions.

Experience the empowerment through an in-person event. Network with experts to accelerate your implementations and efficiently operate your SAP landscape using SAP’s ALM portfolio. A great opportunity to engage with SAP's ALM experts and product management team.

Registration will be opening soon!

entry.title: Dashboard Usage Reporting
entry.content: Hi All. With the upcoming depreciation of Dashboards in SF, has anyone managed to successfully report on their usage? i.e. what Dashboards are used, by who and how often etc so we can identify which, if any will need to be recreated in Story Reports moving forwards.

entry.title: Re: ABAP on BTP Trial Instance creation is getting Failed
entry.content:
Hi

You mentioned that you were successful in creating the instance once.

I recommend changing the region and using different credentials to create an instance now.

Good luck

Thanks

Abhishek Sinha

entry.title: Recruiting - Restricting Job Requisition Creation
entry.content:
Hello,

We are looking for a way to restrict managers from creating job requisitions for higher-level positions. Currently, it is possible for a manager to create a requisition not only for their direct reports but also for positions at the same level or even for their own manager's role.

From a business perspective, the goal is to ensure that managers can only initiate job requisitions for their direct reports—excluding positions above them, at the same level, or their own role.

This requirement is very important for our client, so we would highly appreciate any possible solution. Is there a way to enforce this restriction through permissions or configuration settings? Alternatively, is there an existing rule or at least a workaround that could help achieve this goal?

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Using Job Scheduler in SAP BTP [7]: Multitenancy (0): Intro
entry.content:
Hello @CarlosRoggan ,

thank you for this great blog post, very helpful! Though we currently have this problem: in the TOK2 scenario, when trying to fetch  a JWT token from the tenant-specific xsuaa in order to call the job schedler with that token we have the issue that the tenant-specific xsuaa-tenant of one tenant is fine and delivers the token but for antoher tenant he delivers no token but only responds with "invalid_client", "Bad credentials". 

Another person ( @davischneider ) describes the same problem in his comunity post here: Unable to create Job Scheduler for specific tenant 

Do you have any idea? Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Re: How to configure unauthenticated access to SAP CAP Service for specific UI5 apps?
entry.content: @Hollix How do users access the app? Via Work Zone (managed approuter) or a standalone approuter?

entry.title: Position value not reflecting on job requisition
entry.content:
Hi

We are creating a requisition and position value is not reflecting on the job requisition.

It seems all permissions are correct.

Please can you assist.

 

Thanks,

Nimesha

entry.title: Re: SAP Master Data Governance (Supplier or Customer) - C...
entry.content:
Hi Acram,

MDG BP will always use Internal Standard Grouping for new contact person via customizing 'Define Groupings and Assign Number Ranges' as shown below.

If you are not allowed to change this customizing and want to use another specific BP Grouping for contact person, you have to make some code enhancement to change this default logic.

Br.

SAP Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Drop shipment and returns scenario for Trans...
entry.content:
Hi @ KB786, Could you please provide screen shot how are creating FO. seems you are using standard default FO Types...

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: How to configure unauthenticated access to SAP CAP Se...
entry.content:
@MioYasutake 

Users get access through standalone approuter

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Im Interested.

entry.title: How Are Staging Tables Created in SAP Data Migration Cockpit (DMC)? in Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently working on SAP Data Migration Cockpit (DMC) and trying to understand how staging tables and temporary storage structures are created in the system. While I am aware that staging tables are automatically generated when a migration object is activated in LTMOM (Migration Object Modeler), I would like to gain a deeper understanding of the underlying logic and process.

  •    I have observed that staging tables typically follow the /1LT/<6-digit object ID> pattern

Specific Question:

How does SAP determine the structure of a staging table?

  • What fields are considered, and how does SAP map them from source to target?

    • Where exactly are these tables stored in the HANA database?  

    • Is there a way to manually create or modify staging table structures? Are there specific rules for defining primary keys or mandatory fields

    • What is the reference objects SAP is considering for creating the staging tables?   

Any insights, documentation references, or practical experiences on this topic would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance for your help.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am Interested

DSAG members, click here!

Non-DSAG members, click here!

entry.title: Re: How to create a LaMa repository that provides SPAM/SA...
entry.content: Hi Abe, I'm sorry I somehow missed the notification about your answer. That's the thing, we would still need some kind of central AIX system which provides the mount. We are a data center service provider. So we can't just use any of our AIX (customer) systems to provide a central mount point. That means we would actually have to set up an AIX host that would then simply mount the same Linux file systems that are already attached to LaMa, just so that LaMa recognizes them as AIX. It's not a hugely complex process, of course, but it just seems incredibly unnecessary to me, since it's technically not necessary at all and this dependency is simply created artificially by LaMa. But if there's no other way, then unfortunately, that's the way it is. Thank you for your time and input! I hope you have a great day. Best, Marie

entry.title: Event based revenue recognition -project based sales
entry.content:
Hi,

While creating a  standard sales order against a EPPM project , i want to use same item category for eg. TAN in cost based POC method and completed contract method .

But here, i'm restricted by config node Derivation of recognition key for projects to use only 1 recognition key against the item category.

Also, if the recognition key is already defined at project definition level and we don't want to use the derivation logic for recognition key .Is it possible ?

@LaurenzoC

entry.title: Re: How to see the name of creator of journal in Manage S...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

Unfortunately, there is no way to display the username of the supplier item using the Manage Supplier Item application.

However, this can be displayed using the Manage Journal Entries application :

If you absolutely need to only get the supplier journal entries, you can filter directly on Journal Entry Type field and selecting the ones related to Account Payable's types of documents.

I hope this will help you solve your problem.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Web IDE Service Catalog

entry.title: What are the ABAP release + SP requirements for a connection to S/4HANA 2023 Java ADS?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I was looking for some details about the software requirements to connect an ABAP system (NetWeaver + S/4) to an S/4HANA 2023 Java ADS (Adobe Document Services). But I couldn't find any detailed information in the Guides, Notes or Help Portal documentation.

I'm pretty sure there had been special prerequisites in the past... but I haven't been able to find them again... 🙈

Does anyone here know the requirements regarding ABAP release and SP level and can provide the corresponding source?

Thanks & regards, Marie

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) with SAP Audit Log Viewe
entry.content: Does langGraph rely on Model Context Protocol (MCP) ?

entry.title: Re: How to send Email
entry.content:
Hi

Have you tried creating an email list?

string emailList = string.Join(",", recipientEmails);
Console.WriteLine(emailList);

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Guided Sourcing Event Invitation Process
entry.content:
Hi,

I trust other companies can talk of their own experience here. 

I can share that from a supplier perspective our Supplier dedicated team has made much guidance available in regards to the registration process, as well as the sourcing process, and much more.  FYI they can be guided to our Community page here, which is constantly being updated.  We also run regular webcasts, including a whole series about Sourcing which can be found here on demand (see the 'SAP Ariba Proposals & Questionnaires - Sourcing Series: Registration Process' webcast that is most relevant here). 

Lastly, in case this helpful for suppliers who need further help,  our team also offers consultations for Enterprise suppliers around account management topics such as this – the link is on our Community page (right menu, under ‘Support’).

entry.title: Issue – Unable to use alternative SPL GL Indicator type in Down Payment Request
entry.content:
I am trying to use SPL GL Indicator C, but system throwing the error, GL Code not mapped in SPL GL Indicator “F”, We know we can’t make two primary Key Configuration in Sigle place.

 

So please suggest how to another SPL GL Indicator

 

 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Issue – Unable to use alternative SPL GL Indicator ty...
entry.content:
See kba 3169465 - Special G/L Indicator & not working in Manage Supplier Down Payment Requests App
Which outlines the standard design 

see also best practices in 

Process Navigator - SAP for Me

See also 3452778 - Post from one Down Payment SGI to a different Down Payment SGI

entry.title: We need the provision for FB01 & F-48
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

1) How to post the Journal entry Customer (A) Credit  to customer (B) Debit, - please suggest the app to post the entry

Our client wants to post Customer to Vendor journal entry and Customer to customer journal entry. 

So please suggest how to post these entry in SAP public cloud. 

Upon reviewing the SAP Community, I found that there is no provision for FB01 (Post Document) in the standard SAP configuration

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3428168

2)  SAP system Does not support to Direct down payment (F-48), 

SAP suggest only Downpayment request, however the downpayment request configured only for Spl Ind "F" Our client requirement they want to post Two downpayment for Single Customer, 1st one for Normal Advance another 2nd for Special advance but both are one Reconciliation account, so please suggest how to process this document. 

Thanks

Ragavan

entry.title: Re: 关于中国 balance sheet 和 PL报表的配置问题请教
entry.content: 您是如何管理财务报表的 (Balance sheet & PL)？

entry.title: Re: Completion of third party sales orders
entry.content: Hi, Could you please advise soon? Client needs to complete the orders soon because they need to mark the material as discontinued.

entry.title: Re: Auto generate text for a field
entry.content:
Hi Arun,
Thanks a lot, but need to explore it more because children are not restricted up to 2.

Thanks and Regards

entry.title: Re: Resource Scheduling with SAP Standard (Out-of-the-Box...
entry.content:
Hi Sarvan Kumar,

To be able to dispatch single operations and suboperations individually and level the work center utilization, you need to enhance the core functions of SAP S/4HANA with the additional license for Resource Scheduling.

See the link below for information on the differences between scheduling with SAP S/4HANA asset management for resource scheduling and without: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/e72f747389b340229f7fa343975bfa57/1ffad6fc878e42acb4e2cfd36382b511.html?locale=en-US

Additionally RSH offers the ability to manage work center utilization, use the maintenance scheduling board, manage schedules, plan maintenance work for assets, and much more. See the documentation here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/f2d1705780175c08e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US and a demo video here: https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2023/12/40919e2d-a27e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

Lastly, CM25 is not supported in the Fiori environment. 

Hope this helps,
Ryan

entry.title: Re: How to configure unauthenticated access to SAP CAP Se...
entry.content: @Hollix Is the authentication required when you access the UI, or when you hit the service endpoint after opening the UI?

entry.title: Re: Costing Release Dump
entry.content: Thanks for reply on my query,

entry.title: Exchange of Story Reports among companies that work with Stories already
entry.content:
Dear all,

we are currently working on implementing Story Reports for our different SuccessFactors modules. So far we have been looking into the different Content Store templates as well as attended training sessions and tried to built stories from scratch.

As part of some SAP meetings we have seen that other companies are already more actively working with Story Reports and have shared some of their examples.

I would like to highlight the idea, if it is worthwhile to share stories that have been build by each of you. Such exchange of ideas and if possible sharing of templates was existing in the past for the 'legacy' reporting.

We found that exchange helpful in the past.

Thanks for your support!

All the best,

Mathias

entry.title: Re: Costing Release Dump
entry.content: Issue has been resolved; I have assigned different Costing sheet to Valuation variant.

entry.title: Re: OBYC and KW
entry.content: Just recheck whether the required tolerance limits are maintained in OMRX and OMR6

entry.title: Re: Top 1 for multiple measure in single widget sac
entry.content:
So without a planning model, yep we're left with using text boxes to give the visual impression of a table.
In this example I only used 3 measures just to see if it works.
This should give you better performance and I'm sure my code/logic can be greatly improved to run more efficiently.

The 'table' on the right is 9 text boxes.   
T1,T2,T3    --  T2 is the product, T3 is the value
T4,T5,T6    --  T5 is the product, T6 is the value
T7,T8,T9    --  T8 is the product, T9 is the value

T1,T4,T7 -- I just hardcoded the text in as the values never change

Table_3.rankBy( {  relatedDimensions:{[Alias.MeasureDimension]:"M1"},rankOrder:RankOrder.Top,value:1});

var sel = Table_3.getSelections()[0];
var result = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultMember("Product",sel).description;
T2.applyText("Product"+"  "+result);
var arr = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();
var value=   ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(  arr[0][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue);
var V2=ConvertUtils.numberToString(value);
	{T3.applyText(V2);}


//----------------------------------------------------------------------------

if(T3.getPlainText().length>0)
	{
		Table_3.rankBy( {  relatedDimensions:{[Alias.MeasureDimension]:"M2"},rankOrder:RankOrder.Top,value:1});
sel = Table_3.getSelections()[0];
result = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultMember("Product",sel).description;

T5.applyText("Product"+"  "+result);
arr = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();
value=   ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(  arr[1][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue);
V2=ConvertUtils.numberToString(value);
	T6.applyText(V2);
	
	}


if(T6.getPlainText().length>0)
	
	{
		Table_3.rankBy( {  relatedDimensions:{[Alias.MeasureDimension]:"M3"},rankOrder:RankOrder.Top,value:1});
sel = Table_3.getSelections()[0];
result = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultMember("Product",sel).description;

T8.applyText("Product"+"  "+result);
arr = Table_3.getDataSource().getResultSet();
value=   ConvertUtils.stringToNumber(  arr[2][Alias.MeasureDimension].rawValue);
V2=ConvertUtils.numberToString(value);
	T9.applyText(V2);
	
	}
Table_3.removeRanking();

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Drop shipment and returns scenario for Trans...
entry.content: Yes Ajit,

entry.content:

Sumesh Gupta

entry.title: Old admin accounts deactivation vs jobs and integrations
entry.content:
Hello, we would like to deactivate some old consultants accounts in our system but we see in the  Scheduled Job Manager and Integration Center that those  accounts  are associated  with  some  jobs ( as Job Owner  or Created by ) or integrations (as last modified). What  would  be the  impact  on the  jobs  and  integrations if we deactivate  those old  accounts? What  should we  have in mind  when doing so?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Cancel picking of EWM Production Material Request
entry.content:
Hi @Enzo_Faggella,

I might be wrong, are you looking to clear PSA ( I think same as you explained )?

  • Clear PSA

    After a PMR is completed, with the status Technically Completed, it is possible that there is still a quantity of the product on the PSA, and no other PMR needs the product. In this case, the product must be brought back to the warehouse so it does not occupy space on the PSA.

    You can display the products on the PSA that are not attached to a PMR with the status Released. You can create warehouse tasks to bring the products back to the warehouse.

    For products staged using the single-order staging method, you must release the reference document to clear the reference in the stock. If you do not release the reference document, the stock can never be used for another PMR.

    Source/details: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/019deb535cbb5d1ee10000000a441470.html

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: How to send Email
entry.content: Hi Sinha , Can u please help with 1 complete sample.

entry.title: Re: Position value not reflecting on job requisition
entry.content:
Hi Nimesha,

As long as you use the standard field ID for the position number, the system will automatically map it. If you are using the position object, you need to provide the relevant read/write RBP permissions for the position object. However, if you use the standard free-text position field, RBP is not required.

Can you please share the XML definition of the field ID you are using for the position number in your XML?

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Pricing in delivery note - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public
entry.content:
We are now in a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition project.

In the output form of the delivery note (generated from a sales order), there is no reference to the price—neither for the products nor for the general pricing conditions. The customer needs this delivery note to be valued, including all prices, before sending it to their client. I have seen that at some point there was an option to add a proforma invoice (F8) to the delivery note, but we do not want it to be at the billing level.

What solution can we provide?

Thanks,

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public EditionSAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Drop shipment and returns scenario for Trans...
entry.content: H Ajit, I am creating the flows in Sandbox. For FU I have copied standard 0001 to create 01FU. For FO I have copied 1000 for FO01. Now the flow between 01FU and FO01 is going fine and I have been able to do an end to end process in SAP TM S4 HANA. Now I have created type 5000 FO separately. I am trying to add this 01FU in this FO and it is not happening. Sorry tried to add screenshot but it is not working.

entry.title: Re: Improving SAP Integration Suite with a sorting mechanism for DataStore entries
entry.content:
@Florian_Kube The event mesh capability is a metered/charged feature. The exact details can be found here: https://me.sap.com/notes/2942344, but in essence it states that 'outbound events' are measured as 1 transaction (chargeable message) per 100 events. So this would mean that 'consuming an event from the queue 100 times' would translate to 1 chargeable message (in addition to the 1 message consumed by the iflow itself). The exact implications of that would of course depend on your iflow design.

Regarding the use of APIs, there is no cost associated to calling APIs described on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

entry.title: Slicer Buttons like Feature like PowerBi in SAP Cloud Analytics
entry.content:
Hii community.

i am creating a story where i want to add the button slicer type feature just like in the given image where if i click on running or closed the cards above it increases or decreases in numbers showing more names . Please tell me any work arounds for this. The given image is from PowerBi

entry.title: Is there any way to change the VBRK-VBELN field for an ID that is not being used yet?
entry.content:
As a result of a problem in the configuration of the ranges, the invoice was created with id 249200142R, when in reality it should have been id 259200000R, which has not been used before in the system. Is there any way we can make this change? In other words: Is there any way to change the VRBK-VBELN field for an identification that is not being used and that is prior to the corresponding one that would correspond to this period of the year? If this is not possible, what other solution could be proposed to the client? Any help will be greatly appreciated

Thank you very much in advance. Kind regards

entry.title: Re: How to concatenate value in Global Variable
entry.content:
Hi @Pyae_Phyo_Thwe ,

I have tested in my test environment and it is working fine reading and writing the global variable inside the splitter:

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: We need the provision for FB01 & F-48
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

To answer your first question, I suggest you to use the Post General Journal Entries application with the right journal entry type :

Once you have decided which G/L account to use for posting, make sure it allows the right account type authorized in the document type configuration (SSCUI 101522 - Define Document Types) :

Regarding your second question, you can use the Manage Customer Down Payment Requests app to create the document and then use either the Manage Automatic Payments app or the Post Incoming Payments app to make sure "transforming" your customer down payment request into a customer down payment.

I hope this will help you solve your problems.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Error "The statement ENDCLASS is unexpected"
entry.content: put a period on line 19

entry.title: Show the ID of measures even though it has a description in the builder menue
entry.content:
I have multiple calculations in my story in SAC, each with its own ID. To make the report more readable, I wanted to add descriptions to certain calculations so that viewers can more easily identify the measures. However, I'm accustomed to using the ID names of the calculations. When I add a description, only the description is displayed in the builder menu. Unfortunately, this creates issues, as I cannot find certain calculations anymore since I only remember their ID names.

Is there a way to display the IDs of calculations and measures in the builder menu while still assigning descriptions to certain measures?

To make my issue clearer, here is an example:

I have two measures, and I want a different name to be displayed in the charts. To achieve this, I changed the description of the measures. However, when I update the description, the name in the builder menu also changes, which is problematic.

I hope someone knows a solution to this issue. Otherwise, I even might end up with measures that have the same name

Kind regards
Stefan

entry.title: Registration Connector - migrating registrations of PENDING status
entry.content: I would like to get clarification and understand the behaviour of the LMS system when I run the Registration connector. The client that we are working for are looking to migrate Class data for Classes scheduled to take place in 2nd half of 2025. They have asked me to migrate these Classes into the system and then they want the Class Registrations (i.e Registrations Connector) to be migrated aswell. I have demonstrated to them how the Class connector and Registration connector work. They have raised a question about Registrations that relate to Waitlist statuses. When learners whose Registration status are currently in Waitlist status in their existing LMS, are migrated; what will happen when i migrate these Registrations? How does this impact notifications etc

entry.title: ZR35 Rule error
entry.content: When we run PT60,we are encountering an ZR35 error, Can someone help with this issue.

entry.title: Re: SAP TM - Drop shipment and returns scenario for Trans...
entry.content:
Hi @KB786- "I am trying to add this 01FU in this FO" -> Is this Customer Return FU ? At least copy and paste error so someone can help you.

Note- Go to edit one of your comment then you can see the option.

entry.title: Treffen Sie die Experten des SAP Kundensupports auf den DSAG-Technologietagen 2025 in Wiesbaden
entry.content:
Treffen Sie die Experten des SAP Kundensupports auf den DSAG-Technologietagen 2025 in Wiesbaden

Vom 2. bis 3. April 2025 ist es wieder soweit – die DSAG-Technologietage öffnen ihre Türen in Wiesbaden, und der SAP-Kundensupport ist vor Ort mit spannenden Inhalten und innovativen Ansätzen vertreten! Besuchen Sie den SAP-Stand und lernen Sie die Experten des SAP Kundensupports persönlich kennen.

Was erwartet Sie am SAP-Stand?

Unsere Experten decken eine Vielzahl an Themen ab, die für die Transformation und den Betrieb Ihrer SAP-Lösungen relevant sind:

  • Integrated Toolchain – Umstellung auf die Cloud oder auf eine Clean Core Strategie mit Hilfe von SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio, LeanIX
  • SAP Cloud ALM – Optimieren Sie Ihre Cloud-Reise mit proaktiver Überwachung und Prozessunterstützung
  • SAP Business Transformation Center & Transformation Tools – Tools zur nahtlosen Datenmigration und Systemtransformation
  • AI & Agentic AI im Kundensupport – KI-getriebene Innovationen, die Support-Prozesse revolutionieren
  • SAP for Me & Early Watch Services – Optimieren Sie Ihren Systembetrieb und bleiben Sie proaktiv informiert
  • Black Friday Cyber Monday Readiness Program – Innovative Tools wie Smart Resolve und Cure 360 zur Vorbereitung auf die umsatzstärksten Verkaufsevents des Jahres

Nutzen Sie die Gelegenheit, um mit den SAP-Experten ins Gespräch zu kommen, und erfahren Sie, wie der SAP-Kundensupport als Frontrunner im Einsatz von Künstlicher Intelligenz (KI), Agentic AI und LLMs (Large Language Models) vorangeht. Erhalten Sie Einblicke in den praktischen Einsatz dieser Technologien und nehmen Sie wertvolle Ansätze für Ihr Unternehmen mit.

Verpassen Sie nicht diese spannenden Sessions!

In Ihrem Messefahrplan sollten diese beiden Sessions auf keinen Fall fehlen:

Session 1: SAP TED Talk
🗓2. April, 17:15–18:00 Uhr Sichern Sie Ihre Reise in die Cloud mit SAP Cloud ALM
Planen Sie den Umstieg auf die Cloud? SAP Cloud ALM bietet Ihnen eine klare Roadmap und umfassende Unterstützung für Ihre Cloud-Reise. Diese Lösung minimiert Risiken und stellt einen nahtlosen Betriebsablauf sicher. Entdecken Sie durch praxisnahe Demonstrationen die neuesten Funktionen und erhalten Sie entscheidende Einblicke, um Ihre Cloud-Strategie effektiv zu optimieren. Mit SAP Cloud ALM erhalten Sie nicht nur technische Unterstützung, sondern auch klare Anleitungen für das Projektmanagement, um Ihren Unternehmenserfolg in der digitalen Transformation zu sichern.

Sprecher: Sören Ruder, SVP, Chief Product Officer (CPO) and Head of Customer Experience & Solutions (CXS)

Session 2: SAP Expert Session (freie Q&A)
🗓3. April, 10:55–11:15 Uhr Autonomes Application Lifecycle Management
Die Rolle der Künstlichen Intelligenz (KI), insbesondere der Agentischen KI, beim Transformieren des Application Lifecycle Managements (ALM) steht im Zentrum dieser Präsentation. Erfahren Sie, wie KI-Technologien nicht nur Aufgaben automatisieren, sondern auch proaktiv Entscheidungen treffen, um die Effizienz und Leistungsfähigkeit Ihrer IT-Systeme zu steigern. Erleben Sie, wie autonomes ALM herkömmliche Verwaltungsansätze revolutioniert und den Weg für innovative, zukunftsweisende Prozessgestaltung ebnet.

Sprecher: Sören Ruder, SVP, Chief Product Officer (CPO) and Head of Customer Experience & Solutions (CXS)

Treffen Sie uns auch in den DSAG-Arbeitskreisen

Unsere Experten sind darüber hinaus in den DSAG-Arbeitskreisen aktiv. Ein Blick in die Agenda lohnt sich – vielleicht sehen wir uns dort!

Wir freuen uns auf den persönlichen Austausch mit Ihnen in Wiesbaden. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam über Ihre individuellen Herausforderungen sprechen und wie wir Sie mit modernen Tools, KI-Innovationen und bewährten Services auf Ihrer Reise zur Cloud und darüber hinaus begleiten können.

Mehr Informationen und die vollständige Agenda finden Sie auf der offiziellen Website der DSAG-Technologietage.

entry.title: Re: Understanding SAP’s Product Strategy for Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) Solutions
entry.content:
Hi @ElyasAhmed,
Is SAP GRC 2026 planned to be the last version that will be released?

entry.title: Re: Position value not reflecting on job requisition
entry.content: @Sanjeet2 : PFA the pos definition.The role-Recruiter has the object permissions in miscellaneous permissions.Not sure what else is missing.
